chapter_context.summary: Continues the judgments for three and four transgressions against Moab, Judah (for despising the law), and Israel (the primary target), detailing Israel's systemic social oppression, sexual sins, and ungratefulness for historic deliverances, making their defense useless.

outline.text: Prophecy of Judgment and Fire Against Moab for burning the bones of Edom's king to lime
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Prophecy of Judgment and Fire Against Moab for burning the bones of Edom's king to lime (2:1-3), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Amos.

outline.text: Prophecy of Judgment and Fire Against Judah for despising the law and following lies
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Prophecy of Judgment and Fire Against Judah for despising the law and following lies (2:4-5), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Amos.

outline.text: Fierce Indictment Against Israel: Selling the Righteous for Silver and Oppressing the Poor
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Fierce Indictment Against Israel: Selling the Righteous for Silver and Oppressing the Poor (2:6-8), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Amos.

outline.text: Recalling God's Destruction of the Giants and His Provision of Nazarites and Prophets
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Recalling God's Destruction of the Giants and His Provision of Nazarites and Prophets (2:9-12), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Amos.

outline.text: Impending Absolute Collapse: The Swiftest and Strongest Unable to Escape the Day of Blows
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Impending Absolute Collapse: The Swiftest and Strongest Unable to Escape the Day of Blows (2:13-16), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Amos.

key_themes.title: Social Justice and Equity
key_themes.description: Amos fiercely denounces religious ritual that exists alongside economic exploitation, emphasizing that true worship requires justice for the poor.

key_themes.title: The Danger of Spiritual Complacency
key_themes.description: The book warns that material prosperity can easily blind a nation to its spiritual and ethical decline, leading to swift judgment.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the indictments and visions of Chapter 2, how can you actively champion justice and care for the needy in your community today?
How can we guard ourselves against the kind of complacency and empty religious formalism that Amos rebuked in Samaria?

cultural_historical.title: The Reign of Jeroboam II
cultural_historical.text: Amos prophesied during the mid-8th century BC, a period of remarkable material prosperity, territory expansion, and luxurious stability for the Northern Kingdom under Jeroboam II, which unfortunately saw extreme wealth disparity and systemic social oppression.
